h1: San Jose travel, stays & events guide
heroText: San Jose is a capital city in Costa Rica, combining local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: San Jose
highlights.text: San Jose is a capital city in Costa Rica, combining local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: Founded in 1736 by order of Cabildo de León, its objective was to concentrate the scattered inhabitants of the Aserrí Valley.

history.label: 19th century
history.text: The first modern urban neighborhood carries the name of its founder, the French coffee entrepreneur Monsieur Amon, and was created in the late 19th century, in line with Belle Époque contemporary architecture.
It was not until the enactment of the Constitution of Cádiz in 1812 that San José had its first city government.
On 18 October 1813, the area was first defined as a city by presbyter Florencio del Castillo, on behalf of the Spanish government, a title which was then lost in 1814 when Ferdinand VII.
The municipal government was restored in 1820 along with the title of city and in 1823 San José became the capital of Costa Rica.

history.label: Modern city
history.text: De León thus ordered the construction of a chapel near the area known as La Boca del Monte which was completed a year later.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Unlike neighboring Cartago, San José was not founded by formal decree and thus lacked a city government.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: This makes San José one of the youngest capital cities in Latin America by year of conception.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The accumulation of capital brought by tobacco plantations allowed the city to economically surpass neighboring provinces.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Spurred by the country's improved tourism industry, it is a significant destination and stopover for foreign visitors.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: San José exerts a strong influence because of its proximity to other cities (Alajuela, Heredia and Cartago) and the country's demographic assemblage in the Central Valley. Districts The borders of San José city, as.
